title: How Bangladeshi Freelancers Accept International Payments: How One Creator Went from Zero to Live in 48 Hours — No PayPal Required
author: Unchained Commerce
excerpt: Selling digital products from Nigeria, Ghana, Kenya, Pakistan, or Bangladesh is possible with crypto-native infrastructure that has no geographic restrictions.

content.text: Bangladeshi digital creator, Rohan, was stuck. Despite his talent for creating engaging e-books, he couldn't sell them internationally due to PayPal's restrictions on Bangladesh. This meant missing out on potential customers and revenue. With a goal to go live in 48 hours, Rohan turned to Unchained Commerce for a solution. He needed a way to accept international payments without relying on conventional platforms like PayPal or Stripe.
The Situation Before the Solution

Rohan's struggles were common among digital creators in Bangladesh. PayPal's restrictions made it difficult for them to sell digital products internationally, limiting their access to a global market. This not only hindered their sales but also made it challenging for them to reach a wider audience. With no clear solution in sight, many creators were forced to abandon their online businesses or settle for minimal sales through local marketplaces.
Why Conventional Options Had Already Failed

Rohan had already tried using conventional payment options like PayPal, but they were met with rejection due to Bangladesh's restricted status. He also considered using local payment gateways, but they came with high fees and security concerns. With time running out, Rohan needed a reliable and secure solution that could accept international payments without any hassle. He required a system that was not only efficient but also scalable to meet the demands of his growing business.
The Stack That Was Put Together

Rohan partnered with Unchained Commerce to implement a blockchain-based payment solution. They chose Web3 Wallet Connect, a frontend wallet integration tool that allowed buyers to pay in multiple cryptocurrencies, including BTC, ETH, USDT, SOL, and over 300 more coins. This provided Rohan with the flexibility to accept payments from a global audience while minimizing his reliance on conventional payment processors. The solution also came with a dedicated admin dashboard for easy transaction management and analytics.
The blockchain has no map of restricted countries — a wallet in Lagos processes identically to one in London.
What Happened After Going Live

Within 48 hours, Rohan's e-book store using Unchained Commerce's payment solution was live and open to international buyers. The response was overwhelming, with customers from all over the world purchasing his e-books. Rohan's sales skyrocketed, and he was able to reach a global audience without any restrictions. The success of his store not only boosted his revenue but also gave him the confidence to expand his product line and explore new markets.
The Lessons That Apply to Your Situation

Rohan's story highlights the importance of adapting to the ever-changing landscape of online payments. By embracing blockchain-based solutions, digital creators in Bangladesh can break free from conventional payment restrictions and tap into a global market. With the right tools and expertise, the possibilities are endless, and the future of online commerce looks bright for creators in every corner of the globe.
